description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall forming the main body, topped by a wooden shingled roof supported by sturdy wooden beams. The well features a horizontal wooden crank and spindle mechanism, with a rope wound around the spindle and a wooden bucket attached for drawing water. The stonework is rugged and uneven, giving the well an aged, rustic appearance, while the roof is constructed from overlapping wooden shingles with visible fasteners. The ground around the well is covered with dirt and sparse grass, suggesting an outdoor setting. The well's design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the structure appears robust and weathered from exposure.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden shingled roof, crank mechanism, and bucket, set on a dirt and grass base. The structure is sturdy and functional, with visible aging and weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triangular wooden roof structure, stone well, and a bucket hanging inside the well.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden crank handle, spindle, and the bucket suspended over the well opening.
description_view_2: Rear view with the roof's triangular support and a clear look at the stone wall and bucket inside the well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle on the right and the spindle mechanism, with the bucket visible over the well.
attribute_well_cover_design: A pitched wooden shingled roof supported by wooden beams; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank and spindle mechanism with a rope and wooden bucket suspended over the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high, with a wooden roof supported by vertical beams.
